title: SpecKiwi v1 remediation implementation and test plan

phases.title: Validation, filters, and pagination contract
phases.goal: Close model validation gaps and align list/search filter contracts before cache and release work.

tasks.title: Add PRD item ID duplicate validation
method_signatures: function validatePrdAndTechnicalReferences(workspace: LoadedWorkspace, requirementIds: Set<string>, diagnostics: Diagnostic[]): void
function validatePrdItemIds(document: LoadedSpecDocument, diagnostics: Diagnostic[]): void
tasks.reference_pattern: Use validateRequirements() duplicate Set and firstPathById pattern.
implementation_guide: Iterate PRD items inside each PRD document.
Track item ids per document and emit DUPLICATE_PRD_ITEM_ID on second occurrence.
Keep existing PRD link target validation behavior unchanged.
tasks.rationale: PRD item ids are local trace anchors and must be deterministic inside a PRD document.
pitfalls: Do not make PRD item ids globally unique across different PRD documents.
tasks.test_guidance: Add duplicate failure, cross-document same-id success, and existing link validation regression cases.
dod: DUPLICATE_PRD_ITEM_ID is emitted for duplicate item ids in one PRD
Existing semantic validation tests pass

rollback.command: 1) Restore validation source and tests from git. 2) Remove only the new duplicate PRD fixture file, then run npm test -- validate.

tasks.title: Add project filter to requirement listing
method_signatures: export type ListRequirementsInput = RootInput & PageInput & { project?: string | string[] }
function matchesRequirementFilters(requirement: RegisteredRequirement, input: ListRequirementsInput, registry: RequirementRegistry): boolean
tasks.reference_pattern: Existing scope/type/status/tag filters use splitComma and matchesFilter.
implementation_guide: Add project to Core input DTO and MCP schema.
Add --project to speckiwi list reqs.
Match project id or project name and combine with other filters using AND semantics.
tasks.rationale: The SRS explicitly requires project-based requirement listing filters.
pitfalls: A v1 workspace has one project, so nonmatching project returns an empty page instead of an error.
tasks.test_guidance: Test project id match, project name match, unknown project empty result, and project plus scope/status combination.
dod: CLI, Core, and MCP accept project filter
Project filter composes with status and scope filters

rollback.command: 1) Restore owned Core, CLI, MCP schema, and test files from git. 2) Run npm test -- cli mcp.

tasks.title: Align pagination defaults and limits
method_signatures: function normalizeSearchLimit(value: number | undefined): number
function normalizeListLimit(value: number | undefined): number
tasks.reference_pattern: Current normalizeLimit functions clamp search/list to default 50 and max 200.
implementation_guide: Set search default limit to 10 and max to 100.
Set document and requirement list default limit to 50 and max to 500.
Split MCP page schemas for search and list.
tasks.rationale: Agent pagination must match the readiness contract to avoid missed pages or oversized responses.
pitfalls: Do not apply search max 100 to list APIs.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover search default/max, list default/max, and MCP schema max validation.
dod: Search page limit is 10 by default and 100 max
List page limit is 50 by default and 500 max

rollback.command: 1) Restore pagination-owned files from git. 2) Run npm test -- search cli mcp.

phases.title: Cache read path and no-cache semantics
phases.goal: Connect generated search cache to the read path and enforce cache bypass across read and write flows.

tasks.title: Use valid search cache in search workspace
method_signatures: export function deserializeSearchIndex(value: unknown): SearchIndex | undefined
async function readSearchCache(root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<SearchIndex | undefined>
export async function searchWorkspace(input: SearchInput): Promise<SearchResultSet>
implementation_guide: Add deserializeSearchIndex as the inverse of serializeSearchIndex.
Read search cache when manifest is not stale and cacheMode is not bypass.
Fallback to YAML rebuild path with warning on corrupt cache or missing cache file.
tasks.rationale: SRS performance targets assume cache-based search is part of the runtime path.
pitfalls: Cache read failure must not make YAML source search fail.
tasks.test_guidance: Test cache hit, corrupt cache fallback warning, and stale rebuild.
dod: Search cache hit path is covered
Corrupt cache falls back to YAML search with warning

rollback.command: 1) Restore search/cache owned files from git. 2) Run npm test -- search cache.

tasks.title: Enforce no-cache as full cache read and write bypass
method_signatures: async function applyResolvedProposal(root: WorkspaceRoot, targetStorePath: StorePath, proposal: ProposalDocument, cacheMode: CacheMode): Promise<ApplyResult>
async function markCacheStale(root: WorkspaceRoot, modifiedFiles: string[]): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: rebuildCache() already bypasses writes when cacheMode is bypass.
implementation_guide: Pass input.cacheMode into applyResolvedProposal.
Skip markCacheStale when cacheMode is bypass.
Verify search, cache, list, graph, and export paths do not perform cache read/write in bypass mode.
Test apply auto mode still creates stale marker.
tasks.rationale: The no-cache contract covers both reads and writes, including stale marker creation.
pitfalls: Bypass must not skip the YAML apply itself.
tasks.test_guidance: Test apply auto stale marker, apply bypass no stale marker, search bypass no cache file writes, graph bypass no cache file writes, and export bypass no cache file writes.
dod: Bypass mode does not create cache files or stale markers during apply/search/graph/export
Auto mode stale marker behavior remains covered

rollback.command: 1) Restore write/cache/cli test files from git. 2) Run npm test -- write cache cli.

phases.title: Cross-process apply locking
phases.goal: Make same-target apply locking work across CLI processes and recover from stale locks.

tasks.title: Replace in-memory apply lock with file-based target lock
method_signatures: export async function withTargetWriteLock<T>(root: WorkspaceRoot, target: StorePath, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>
async function acquireFileLock(root: WorkspaceRoot, target: StorePath): Promise<WriteLockHandle>
tasks.reference_pattern: Current lock uses module-scope Set and only protects one process.
implementation_guide: Create target lock file with exclusive create under .speckiwi/.locks.
Treat .speckiwi/.locks as apply safety state, not cache.
Use a hash of store path for lock filename.
Return APPLY_REJECTED_LOCK_CONFLICT on active lock conflict.
tasks.rationale: Separate CLI node processes do not share memory.
pitfalls: cacheMode bypass must not disable apply locking.
Do not place lock files under .speckiwi/cache because that conflicts with the no-cache contract.
tasks.test_guidance: Spawn two node processes against the same target and assert exactly one succeeds.
dod: Cross-process same-target apply has one success and one deterministic conflict

rollback.command: 1) Restore src/write/lock.ts, src/write/apply.ts, and write tests from git. 2) Run npm test -- write.

tasks.title: Add stale lock recovery and cleanup tests
method_signatures: function isStaleLock(payload: WriteLockPayload, now: Date): boolean
async function releaseFileLock(handle: WriteLockHandle): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: Apply already checks stale proposal before and after validation.
implementation_guide: Treat malformed lock payload or expired TTL as stale.
Remove stale lock and retry acquire.
Assert successful apply removes its target lock.
tasks.rationale: Lock recovery prevents a terminated process from blocking apply indefinitely.
pitfalls: Do not use an aggressive TTL that can expire during a valid apply.
tasks.test_guidance: Test precreated stale lock success, active lock conflict, and success cleanup.
dod: Stale lock is recoverable
Normal apply leaves no target lock file

rollback.command: 1) Restore lock and hardening test files from git. 2) Run npm test -- write hardening.

phases.title: MCP security and protocol contract
phases.goal: Block symlink traversal and expose a clearer MCP protocol contract.

tasks.title: Reject symlink traversal on Core and MCP reads
method_signatures: export async function resolveRealStorePath(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: StorePath): Promise<WorkspacePath>
export async function assertRealPathInsideWorkspace(path: WorkspacePath): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: resolveStorePath currently performs lexical containment with isInsideDirectory.
implementation_guide: Add realpath-based containment checks.
Use checked path for Core readDocument, YAML loader, proposal/apply write targets, and MCP resources.
Reject apply before atomic write when the real target path escapes .speckiwi.
Assert symlink to external temp file never returns or modifies external content.
tasks.rationale: Symlink target traversal can bypass lexical path checks.
pitfalls: Keep normalizeStorePath as a string policy and perform realpath checks separately.
tasks.test_guidance: Test overview symlink read rejection, registered document symlink read rejection, apply target symlink write rejection, and normal regular file read/write.
dod: MCP and CLI read paths reject workspace-external symlink targets
Apply write path rejects workspace-external symlink targets before writing

rollback.command: 1) Restore path, document, MCP resource, and test files from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp hardening.

tasks.title: Return invalid params for MCP input shape errors
method_signatures: function registerStrictTool<T>(server: McpServer, name: string, config: ToolConfig<T>, handler: (input: T) => Promise<CallToolResult>): void
function toInvalidParams(error: unknown): McpError
tasks.reference_pattern: Current root override test receives isError result from callTool.
implementation_guide: Separate input schema failures from domain Core failures.
Assert root override is observed as invalid params.
Keep apply policy rejection as structuredContent error result.
tasks.rationale: Clients need to distinguish malformed tool input from normal domain failure.
pitfalls: Do not throw for every Core ErrorResult.
tasks.test_guidance: Test root override invalid params, unknown field invalid params, and apply allowApply false domain error.
dod: MCP invalid params and domain errors have distinct observable behavior

rollback.command: 1) Restore MCP tool/schema tests and source from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp.

tasks.title: Add MCP output schemas for structured content
method_signatures: export const machineResultOutputSchema: z.ZodType
function toolOutputSchemaFor(name: string): unknown
tasks.reference_pattern: Tool registration currently includes inputSchema and annotations only.
implementation_guide: Define a common MachineResult output schema.
Attach outputSchema to all registered tools.
Test listTools metadata and envelope compatibility.
tasks.rationale: Tool metadata should describe structuredContent for agents.
pitfalls: Do not overfit per-tool schemas before the common envelope is stable.
tasks.test_guidance: Test outputSchema exists for all 13 tools and matches success/error envelopes.
dod: All speckiwi_* tools expose outputSchema metadata

rollback.command: 1) Restore MCP output schema source and tests from git. 2) Run npm test -- mcp.

phases.title: Release and performance gates
phases.goal: Make release and performance checks catch the remediation risks they are meant to guard.

tasks.title: Include release acceptance in release check
method_signatures: export function releaseCommands(): ReleaseCommand[]
export async function runReleaseCheck(options?: RunReleaseCheckOptions): Promise<number>
implementation_guide: Add release acceptance command to releaseCommands.
Set explicit timeout for slow acceptance tests.
Update command sequence expectations.
tasks.rationale: Release gate must fail when release acceptance fails.
pitfalls: Avoid making release acceptance timeout rely on machine speed defaults.
tasks.test_guidance: Test command sequence, timeout stability, and failure propagation.
dod: npm test includes release acceptance without timeout failure
npm run release:check includes release acceptance

rollback.command: 1) Restore scripts/release-check.mjs, package.json, and release tests from git. 2) Run npm test -- release.

tasks.title: Expand performance tests to SRS scale targets
method_signatures: async function buildLargeWorkspaceFixture(input: { requirementCount: number; documentCount?: number }): Promise<PerfFixture>
async function measureMcpToolCall(root: string, toolName: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<number>
tasks.reference_pattern: Current perf fixture defaults to 600 requirements.
implementation_guide: Keep default perf smoke fast.
Add env-driven SRS scale mode for 10000 requirements and 1000 documents.
Expose the SRS-scale run through npm run perf:srs or an equivalent package script.
Measure MCP single tool call under normal cache state.
tasks.rationale: SRS performance targets need direct measurable coverage.
pitfalls: Do not make normal npm test too slow.
SRS-scale direct validation must still be runnable by a named command, not only by undocumented environment variables.
tasks.test_guidance: Cover default perf smoke, named SRS-scale perf command, env-driven scale mode, and MCP call timing.
dod: Default perf test passes quickly
npm run perf:srs or an equivalent named command directly measures the SRS-scale targets

rollback.command: 1) Restore perf tests and any touched core/MCP files from git. 2) Run npm test -- perf.

phases.title: Integration regression and documentation alignment
phases.goal: Prove all remediation requirements through a final acceptance matrix and update user-facing docs.

tasks.title: Add remediation acceptance matrix and final regression gate
method_signatures: export async function assertV1Acceptance(root: string): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: Existing release acceptance runs core CLI commands against a fixture workspace.
implementation_guide: Add an acceptance assertion for each remediation checklist item.
Update README examples for user-facing filters and no-cache behavior.
Trace SRS 34.6 checklist items through test names or assertions without changing docs/spec/srs.md semantics.
Include the SRS-scale performance command in release documentation or release-check flow if it was added.
Run full pre-commit gate and route failures back to the owning phase.
tasks.rationale: Final release acceptance should trace every remediation requirement.
pitfalls: Do not change SRS semantics while documenting implementation behavior.
tasks.test_guidance: Add one assertion per SRS 34.6 checklist item in release acceptance or mapped test names.
dod: Every SRS 34.6 checklist item is mapped to automated coverage
Full pre-commit gate passes

rollback.command: 1) Restore README, release acceptance, and package script edits owned by this task from git. 2) Run npm run release:check to confirm rollback state.

risks.risk: Windows file lock behavior differs from POSIX
risks.mitigation: Use exclusive file create semantics and keep stale detection conservative.

risks.risk: SRS-scale perf tests can slow normal test runs
risks.mitigation: Keep default perf smoke small and make SRS-scale opt-in through env vars.

risks.risk: MCP SDK outputSchema and invalid params surface can differ by version
risks.mitigation: Assert the SDK-observed shape and keep domain errors in structuredContent.

risks.risk: Symlink creation can be unavailable on some Windows environments
risks.mitigation: Capability-detect symlink tests and backstop with realpath helper unit tests.
